rows.title: Bounded trust-region robust search around the UA3 incumbent
rows.setup_note: Implemented and ran a bounded adaptive trust-region BO controller in the existing lumo environment. The controller preserved the active comparison contract: baseline ax-pybamm-fastcharge with headline comparator BO_3step_aggressive, required paper-facing metrics Q30/plating_loss/sei_growth/total_lli, and the same five-scenario followup_v1 robustness bundle used by the incumbent line.
rows.result_summary: The mechanism upgrade was technically successful: the trust-region BO controller ran end-to-end, wrote full ranked outputs, and produced adaptive candidates under the unchanged evaluation contract. The scientific result was neutral. The robust winner remained UA3_3.00C_rest10m_3.80C, while the nominal-only winner remained BO_3step_conservative. Across 70 scenario evaluations, all candidates simulated successfully but 34 evaluations failed the robust guard, showing that the main bottleneck is feasibility-aware candidate quality rather than simulator execution. The closest safe adaptive candidate, UA3_TRBO_3.00C_rest10m_3.75C, passed all guards but was still slightly worse than the incumbent on the robust score.

rows.title: Feasibility-aware trust-region robust search beyond the UA3 incumbent
rows.setup_note: Implemented a feasibility-aware upgrade of the previous trust-region BO controller in the existing lumo environment. The new controller preserved the active comparison contract: baseline ax-pybamm-fastcharge with headline comparator BO_3step_aggressive, required metrics Q30/plating_loss/sei_growth/total_lli, and the same five-scenario followup_v1 robustness bundle. The mechanism change was localized to the proposal rule: a dual-model controller learned both search_objective and guard_pass_rate, then used guard-feasibility-weighted expected improvement to pick new candidates on the same three-parameter protocol surface.
rows.result_summary: The feasibility-aware controller produced a real scientific gain. The new robust winner is UA3_TRBO_3.00C_rest10m_3.95C, not the old incumbent UA3_3.00C_rest10m_3.80C. On the nominal surface it improved Q30 from 0.0926797696 to 0.0933852046 while preserving plating_loss and further reducing sei_growth and total_lli. In the bounded main search, the top four adaptive candidates above the old incumbent all kept guard_pass_rate 1.0, and the new winner achieved mean_score 0.0222770421 with worst_score 0.0 and robust_utility 0.0129961619. This is the first line in the project where the mechanism upgrade itself, not just local manual enumeration, materially improves the incumbent.

rows.title: Frontier-anchored feasibility-aware trust-region search beyond the 3.95C winner
rows.setup_note: Used the existing lumo CUDA environment. Reused the ua_trbo_v1 package but changed the active anchor to UA3_TRBO_3.00C_rest10m_3.95C, narrowed the first-rate/rest-time neighborhood, extended third-stage current search up to 4.15C, and biased candidate sampling toward the local upper frontier while keeping the same four-metric comparison contract and robust guard-first ranking rule.
rows.result_summary: The main frontier-followup search confirmed UA3_TRBO_3.00C_rest10m_4.15C as the new robust top candidate. Relative to the 3.95C anchor, the nominal metrics improved to Q30=0.09440408660083764, plating_loss=0.3218373881357771, sei_growth=57.57659506258706, and total_lli=0.014026363883363733. Across the full followup_v1 bundle the winner kept guard_pass_rate=1.0, and the search controller converged at best_point=[3.0,10.0,4.15] with stop_reason=trust_region_floor.

rows.title: Voltage-aware tapered proxy search beyond the 4.15C incumbent
rows.setup_note: Used the existing lumo CUDA environment and the new ua_vtbo_v1 package on the dedicated run branch. The search family exposed four parameters: first_rate, rest_minutes, third_rate, and tail_rate, while keeping a fixed 3-minute tail window as the first voltage-aware proxy. The comparison contract, guard-first ranking rule, and baseline references were unchanged.
rows.result_summary: The robust top protocol was UA4_VTBO_3.00C_rest10m_4.25C_tail2.40C. Its nominal metrics were Q30=0.09494306799062802 Ah, plating_loss=0.3218373881357771 Ah, sei_growth=57.547978013417904 nm, and total_lli=0.014025360821606166 mol. Relative to the prior 4.15C incumbent, nominal Q30 improved by +0.0005389813897903745 Ah (+0.57%), plating_loss stayed equal, sei_growth improved by -0.028617049169156417 nm, and total_lli improved by -1.003061757565496e-06 mol. Relative to BO_3step_aggressive, nominal Q30 improved by +0.005048054532459795 Ah (+5.62%), plating_loss improved by -0.00033812742771810456 Ah, sei_growth improved by -0.8141897102389706 nm, and total_lli improved by -4.115436317315112e-05 mol. The robust aggregate for the winner was success_rate=1.0, guard_pass_rate=1.0, mean_score=0.0217539631976511, worst_score=0.0, and robust_utility=0.01263747343014421.

rows.title: UA_EVTBO v1 bounded robust search on followup_v1
rows.setup_note: Used the accepted ax-pybamm-fastcharge baseline contract in the `lumo` environment, extended with backward-compatible native PyBaMM charge-step support. The main run evaluated the ua_evtbo_v1 guard-then-trigger protocol family against the current incumbent UA4_VTBO_3.00C_rest10m_4.25C_tail2.40C under the followup_v1 five-scenario robust bundle.
rows.result_summary: The run selected UA4_EVTBO_3.00C_rest10m_4.30C_v4p16 as the robust top candidate. Relative to the current incumbent, it achieved success_rate=1.0 and guard_pass_rate=1.0, nominal Q30 0.0952188872 vs 0.0949430680, warm-cell Q30 0.0975657353 vs 0.0974793778, and hot-cell Q30 0.0991951788 vs 0.0992325685. Plating loss, SEI growth, and total LLI were improved or unchanged in every scenario where the candidate differed from the incumbent. Relative to baseline BO_3step_aggressive, the new candidate ranked first while the baseline ranked ninth with guard_pass_rate 0.4 and much worse robust utility.

rows.title: PSMS-v2 larger-budget robust search beyond the EVTBO anchor
rows.setup_note: Use the accepted PyBaMM fast-charge baseline contract on the existing lumo environment. Search on the followup_v1 five-scenario bundle with the EVTBO incumbent UA4_EVTBO_3.00C_rest10m_4.30C_v4p16 as the anchor. PSMS-v2 adds a separate entry-rate dimension and a sequence-feasibility prior inside the trust-region acquisition.
rows.result_summary: The main experiment promoted UA5_PSMS_3.00C_rest10m_4.30C_e4.50_v4p18_h3.35_1p5m as the robust top protocol. It kept success_rate=1.0 and guard_pass_rate=1.0 across the five followup_v1 scenarios while improving nominal Q30 to 0.09636 Ah versus 0.09522 Ah for the EVTBO anchor and 0.08990 Ah for the accepted baseline. Relative to the EVTBO anchor, the winner improved mean Q30 by +0.00358, plating_loss by +0.09569, sei_growth by +0.00111, and total_lli by +0.06517 on the accepted relative-delta surface, with worst-case deltas staying non-negative on the four canonical metrics.

rows.title: PSMS-v3 formal search with a learned feasibility gate
rows.setup_note: Used the same UA4 anchor family and followup_v1 scenario set as the PSMS-v2 larger-budget search, but enabled the lightweight learned feasibility gate discovered in smoke tests. The formal search used search_budget=12 and preserved the confirmed comparison metrics Q30, plating_loss, sei_growth, and total_lli.
rows.result_summary: The formal PSMS-v3 run did not improve on PSMS-v2. It produced the same robust top protocol as the prior PSMS-v2 larger-budget search: UA5_PSMS_3.00C_rest10m_4.30C_e4.50_v4p18_h3.35_1p5m. The top-ranked table and summary metadata matched PSMS-v2 exactly, including robust_utility=0.023824166035076547, mean_score=0.04138956386002244, mean_delta_Q30=0.0035845855267959232, and adaptive_candidates_evaluated=11. The learned prior was active and trained on 86 rows (33 feasible, 53 infeasible), but it did not change the final ranking outcome.

rows.title: PSMS-v5 warm-start candidate validation against the PSMS-v2 incumbent
rows.setup_note: Used the accepted baseline contract `ax-pybamm-fastcharge` / `bo_3step_aggressive` and rebuilt the two warm-start candidates from stored search points in `ua_psms_v5_warmstart_smoke12_summary.json`. Repaired the focused validation runner so it reuses the shared parameter-update path and only passes `charge_steps` when the simulator signature supports it.
rows.result_summary: Full followup_v1 validation finished cleanly for three protocols. The incumbent `UA5_PSMS_3.00C_rest10m_4.30C_e4.50_v4p18_h3.35_1p5m` stayed robust-top with `guard_pass_rate=1.0` and `worst_score=0.0`. The conservative warm-start candidate `UA5_PSMS_3.00C_rest11m_4.30C_e4.40_v4p17_h3.50_1m` reached `guard_pass_rate=0.4`, `worst_score=-0.04108363187839041`, `robust_utility=0.119607248106129`, `mean_delta_Q30=0.5812408542161849`, and `mean_delta_total_lli=0.11577338364283643`. The promoted acquisition candidate `UA5_PSMS_3.00C_rest12m_4.30C_e4.35_v4p17_h3.50_1m` became nominal-top but still failed the robust gate with `guard_pass_rate=0.4`, `worst_score=-0.05340962833828226`, `robust_utility=0.1636056954924982`, `mean_delta_Q30=0.635357191521795`, and `mean_delta_total_lli=0.20900507638169738`. Both warm-start candidates failed guard checks in `hot_cell`, `plating_stress`, and `sei_stress`, so this validation downgrades the warm-start line from promotion-ready evidence back to exploratory evidence.
